transcript_segments.text: Today we're making smash burgers which are a super violent burger with a very destructive taste. We're going to start by taking out some unsmashed beef. You can buy the pre-smashed stuff if you want but it won't taste nearly as wrecked. We've got a pound here with around 20% fat. You can use the super lean stuff if you want but it might cause you to lose too much weight so be very careful. We'll divide this into six even-ish beef balls.

Hey, your beef. Start acting like it. As far as toppings go, I'm just gonna go with what I have here. Nothing fancy. I've got a nice dank onion, some nice hood tomatoes. Let's just check inside here and hopefully, oh yeah, pickles. That's awesome. To me, this is essential. And then we'll just peel off a couple slices of cupboard cheese.

I think that's it, but let's just check and see if there's any, oh, okay. Hey, listen, this is kind of hard to say, but people are just using you when they put you on a burger to try to make it seem more healthy by including salad. All you really do is get soggy and you taste like nothing. So I just wanted you to know the truth.

transcript_segments.text: Okay great. We're gonna make fresh potato buns which we'll do by taking a cup of flour, half tablespoon of sugar, one teaspoon of instant yeast, half teaspoon of salt, two cups of water, and two potatoes. Then we'll grab those potatoes and just smash them against the wall. If you do it right you'll get this nice crack right in the middle of the buns.

Oh look, it's like a miniature man tried to run through a cartoon wall. If you slice onions thin enough, they won't make you cry, provided you also have no tear ducts. And we'll slice up these magical flavor strips. Do not make a burger without them. If you don't like pickles, one thing you can do is seek help. Then we'll slice up the tomato. Okay, crispy water, you can join this once, but don't get cocky.

Now before we start cooking, we'll do a final check to make sure we have everything, which is known as mise en place, which is a French term for get all your together before you start. I've got the buns here, I've got my beef balls, I've got my sliced pickle ready to go, I've got the onions here, and I've got that shredded crispy water, sliced tomato, and I've got my cheese. So it looks like we're ready.

We're gonna grill our buns in some butter in advance so they're ready to go, which uses the exact same process as when you make a grilled butter sandwich.

For our burger sauce, I'm gonna take some mayo and hot sauce and wang jangle that together. I'm putting the sauce on both sides of the bun. Condimenting only one side of a bun is a certifiable burger crime. We need moisture coming from all directions, or at least two directions. We've got our cast iron griddle heating up, and you wanna crank that all the way up to medium. If you let it heat up for a full 10 minutes on medium, it's gonna be plenty hot. But if you wanna brag to your buddies later about how hot your pan is, go ahead and floor it.
